analysis.summary: Zulip is an open-source team chat application built on Django with topic-based threading. It processes real-time messages, manages user authentication, handles file uploads, and provides REST/event APIs for chat functionality.
capabilities: Processes incoming chat messages and organizes them by streams and topics (zerver/models/message.py, zerver/views/messages.py)
Manages user authentication including LDAP, SAML, and social auth (zerver/views/auth.py, zerver/lib/auth.py)
Handles real-time message delivery via websockets and tornado server (zerver/tornado/*, zerver/lib/events.py)
Processes file uploads with S3 and local storage backends (zerver/lib/upload.py)
Renders markdown messages with custom extensions (zerver/lib/markdown/)
Manages user permissions and realm-based access control (zerver/lib/permissions.py)
Provides REST API endpoints for all chat operations (zerver/views/*, documented in api_docs/)
Analytics tracking for message counts and user activity (analytics/lib/counts.py)
Email notifications and digest generation (zerver/lib/send_email.py)
Mobile push notifications via FCM and APNS (zerver/lib/push_notifications.py)
Webhook integrations for external services (zerver/webhooks/*/)
Bot framework for automated message processing (zerver/lib/bot_lib.py)
technical_stack: RabbitMQ for message queuing (tools/ci/Dockerfile mentions rabbitmq)
Webpack for frontend build (package.json webpack dependencies)
integrations: AWS S3 for file storage (boto3 dependency, zerver/lib/upload.py)
AWS SNS/SQS for notifications (mypy-boto3-sns, mypy-boto3-sqs dependencies)
External webhooks system (zerver/webhooks/ directory with 100+ integrations)
Sentry for error tracking (@sentry/browser in package.json)

api_endpoints.summary: Register event queue for real-time events (zerver/views/events_register.py)

technical_facts: Processes incoming chat messages and organizes them by streams and topics (zerver/models/message.py, zerver/views/messages.py)
Manages user authentication including LDAP, SAML, and social auth (zerver/views/auth.py, zerver/lib/auth.py)
Handles real-time message delivery via websockets and tornado server (zerver/tornado/*, zerver/lib/events.py)
Processes file uploads with S3 and local storage backends (zerver/lib/upload.py)
Renders markdown messages with custom extensions (zerver/lib/markdown/)
Manages user permissions and realm-based access control (zerver/lib/permissions.py)
Provides REST API endpoints for all chat operations (zerver/views/*, documented in api_docs/)
Analytics tracking for message counts and user activity (analytics/lib/counts.py)
Email notifications and digest generation (zerver/lib/send_email.py)
Mobile push notifications via FCM and APNS (zerver/lib/push_notifications.py)

architecture_patterns: Concurrency: async via Tornado for WebSockets and real-time events, Django's synchronous request handling for HTTP API
deployment_options: CI/CD: GitHub Actions (visible in README badges and .github/ would be expected)

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: Manages user authentication including LDAP, SAML, and social auth (zerver/views/auth.py, zerver/lib/auth.py) and Manages user permissions and realm-based access control (zerver/lib/permissions.py) - orchestrates multiple services and maintains state across chat sessions
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Django 5.2 (pyproject.toml) and PostgreSQL (Dockerfile-postgresql, zerver/lib/db.py) - Django ORM enforces strict database schemas
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Processes incoming chat messages and organizes them by streams and topics (zerver/models/message.py, zerver/views/messages.py) and Provides REST API endpoints for all chat operations (zerver/views/*, documented in api_docs/) - primarily database operations for message storage and retrieval
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Handles real-time message delivery via websockets and tornado server (zerver/tornado/*, zerver/lib/events.py) and Redis for caching (tools/ci/Dockerfile, zerver/lib/redis_utils.py) - real-time chat requires eventual consistency across distributed clients
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: High repurposing potential due to modular Django architecture, well-defined REST APIs, and generic messaging/notification infrastructure. The authentication, file upload, and real-time event systems are highly transferable to other domains requiring collaborative features.

evidence_claims.claim: Built on Django framework with PostgreSQL database

evidence_claims.evidence: Handles real-time message delivery via websockets and tornado server (zerver/tornado/*, zerver/lib/events.py)
Manages user authentication including LDAP, SAML, and social auth (zerver/views/auth.py, zerver/lib/auth.py)
Provides REST API endpoints for all chat operations (zerver/views/*, documented in api_docs/)

information_gaps: Database migration complexity for schema changes
